observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Accounting Office to: (i) dig up the source documents pertaining to the liabilities recorded in the general/subsidiary ledgers and determine liabilities that need to be settled by preparation of appropriate adjusting entries or return of remaining funds to source agencies; (ii) furnish COA with the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) taking up the adjusting/correcting entries relative to the accounts Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities; and (iii) maintain records and source documents necessary for the proper monitoring and timely settlement of liabilities; and

The Municipal Treasurer to return to the Source Agency any unutilized funds that are no longer needed as stipulated in the MOA governing the disposition of any balance thereof after the completion of the specific programs and projects pursuant to Item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, otherwise the unutilized balance will be transferred from Trust Fund to the General Fund pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended to require the Human Resource and Management Officer to prioritize the hiring of the Local DRRM Officer II and Local DRRM Assistant positions to ensure comprehensive implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management as required under RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

Direct the NGOs/POs and the Municipal Accountant to submit to Commission on Audit a Status Report on the Implementation of the Project and a satisfactory written explanation as to why liquidations of funds transferred to NGOs/POs remained outstanding in the books of accounts over the last four years; and

Demand from the NGOs/POs to return the unutilized fund/ excess amount of funds to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct a reconciliation of records to establish a realistic balance of RPT and SET Receivables. Appropriate adjustments shall be made in the books for the discrepancies noted, amounting to ₱83,422.07; and
Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with an updated certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for CY 2025. This list shall serve as the basis for setting up the RPT and SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT and SET Income at the beginning of the succeeding year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts under the three funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error or misstatements in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant  refrain from using the account “Donations” for training and travel-related expenses incurred in the performance of official duties and record the actual expenses based on the accurate classification of financial transactions as documented and supported by accounting records pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Unit as described in Section 5.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee through the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of all contracts/purchase orders irrespective of amounts, and each and every supporting documents forming part thereof to the Audit Team within five working days from the date of execution or issuance thereof as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and
Instruct the General Services Officer to notify in writing and furnished the Auditor concerned with copies of AIR within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods and services in accordance with Item 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Management continue to allocate at least 20 percent of the Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects and continue to utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the purpose for which the fund was established;
The Local Development Council (LDC) to evaluate the necessity of the identified priority PPAs which to date, remained unimplemented, conduct procurement planning to ensure that PPAs are procurement and implementation ready, set specific timeline for completion, and assign personnel responsible to oversee the implementation; and
The Local Development Council (LDC) to consider reprogramming/re-aligning the accumulated savings totaling ₱9.797 million to other development projects which should be embodied in the local development plan, and medium-term and annual investment program of the succeeding year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Municipal Mayor to instruct the assigned LDRRMO, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Local DRRM Council to prepare and furnish the Audit Team with a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMIP that includes not only current appropriation but also those PPAs under the continuing appropriations and the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF under the Special Trust Fund, as required under Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, and Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2002;
Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to regularly submit to the Auditor the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
Management take full advantage of the LDRRM fund through optimal utilization of the funds provided for programs and projects designed and intended to build a disaster-resilient community.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following courses of actions:
The Designated GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required PC, RPLC and PPELC, respectively per category of PPE; and
Reconciliation of balances between the PC, RPLC and PPELC should be regularly made by the concerned offices to properly establish custodianship and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following courses of action:
Local Chief Executive to provide budgetary allocation for the purchase of biometric scanner/bundy clock and implement its use as the official method for recording daily attendance of all personnel of the municipality; and
Compute salaries and wages based on the DTR generated by the biometric scanner/bundy clock.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Accounting staff in charge of Pag-IBIG remittance to trace and remit in full the unremitted balances of ₱84,636.23.

Perform the necessary reconciliation and instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to trace the previous years’ balance of ₱96,921.80 and cause its immediate remittance to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies. Further, maintain and review postings to the respective subsidiary ledgers (SLs) and reconcile/adjust the differences immediately.

Management stop the practice of charging expenditures against the five percent LDRRM fund that are not supportive of the key objectives of disaster risk reduction and management; and

Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the LDRRM fund be strictly utilized for the implementation of projects and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation, and recovery.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Treasurer to render his accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with the supporting documents to the Accounting Office, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.

Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 in relation to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.

We recommended and the Management agreed to prepare and cause the immediate submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for COA AAR for CY 2022 using the attached prescribed format, henceforth observe strict compliance to COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated March 18, 2014.

1.6 We recommended the following to management:

a) All AOs remit/deposit all collections intact daily or at the close of each business day and for collectors assigned to the field, where travel time from their places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00; and

b)The Liquidating Officer perform proper verification procedures as prescribed in the manual and sign only the RCD after checking that all entries are correct and properly verified with;

c)The Accountable Form Custodian only release new accountable forms after ensuring that previously issued accountable forms are properly accounted for and collections from which is duly remitted to the liquidating officer; and

d) Require in writing all AOs with unremitted collections to immediately remit the same to the liquidating officer. For the unremitted amount P2,610,087.87, the same was already filed as shortage.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office implement the following:

a. Increase the bond of the AO commensurate to his accountability, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources if necessary; and

b. Ensure that the accountable officer remit their collections before reaching the maximum cash accountability.

We recommended that management judiciously plan the procurement process of the agency and prepare an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) including Infrastructure Projects in compliance with Article II, Section 7 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Malinao Waterworks System Department Head as well as his staff should exert more effort in intensifying collections from waterworks system being one of the economic enterprises of the municipality in order to make it profitable and self-reliant. Information dissemination and confrontation with the water consumers/ households with the help of the barangay officials should be done in order to explain the give and take relationship between the municipal government and its inhabitants in the continuous usage of public utilities such as water system.

Also, the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan members should make policies on cost-cutting in the Malinao Waterworks System to adapt with very little income of waterworks system and avoid losses considering that the Local Government Unit of Malinao invested so much in providing potable water to the inhabitants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management that to direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all remedies to recover records/ documents available to validate claims or else file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund and prepare the financial reports with necessary supporting schedules. Separate financial statements for the development projects shall likewise be prepared at year-end to form part of the schedule of the General Fund Trial Balance as mandated under Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 110 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Year-end FS for all funds, with complete schedules and annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with the abovementioned existing rules and regulations; and
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as allowing the Accounting personnel to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory financial reports, pursuant to mandated deadlines.

We recommended that management require the accountable officers to prepare and submit the Monthly RAAFs not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month in accordance with the NGAS. We also recommended that the accountable officer produce to the audit team the duplicate copies and RCD for the unreported OR series.
We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officer to observe the proper handling of Cashbook as prescribed under Section 26 of the NGAS and reconcile the noted differences.

We recommended that Management:
Constitute an Inventory Committee/Team to conduct inventory of the Municipality’s properties;
Require the Inventory Committee / Team to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of the agency’s properties in accordance with Sections 490 and 491 of GAAM, Volume I, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form as shown in Annex 49 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Property Officer / General Services Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, respectively and conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, through BAC Secretariat, to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP and PMR in accordance with the pertinent provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality comply with the posting requirements and submission to the GPPB to ensure transparency and integrity in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management adhere to the procurement and disbursement process prescribed under Republic Act No. 9184, NGAs for LGU Volume I, and COA Circular No. 97-002 and require the direct payment to the suppliers for the purchases made to ensure correct and prompt remittance of withheld taxes due to the government.

We recommended that Management require the personnel assigned in preparing the PR, PO, IAR and RFQ to accomplish said forms completely and accurately not only to facilitate verification and monitoring but more significantly, to strengthen the internal control on procurement.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the prescribed procurement guidelines and procedures in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  A Notice of Disallowance will be issued for the above-mentioned transactions.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned personnel to require the contractor to refund the amount of ₱371,376.63 representing the 10% retention or to post a surety bond/other applicable instrument as provided under the said regulations.  Henceforth, management ensure the strict compliance with the procurement laws.

We recommended that Management:
Plan thoroughly the budget for the 20 per cent DF and pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the implementation of development projects to ensure that optimum benefits are derived by the greater number of its constituents;
Meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Plan and provide realistic timelines for its completion; and
Observe proper timing in the implementation of the infrastructure projects to give ample time for the implementing office in the prompt accomplishment of the projects.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Solid Waste Management Board and all the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation in the revision and finalization of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU to be able to comply with the requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission.
Also, create a separate plantilla position for Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to oversee the duties and functions of the office.

We recommended that Management, through the LSB, prepare SEF Annual Budget on time in accordance with Section 272 of the Local Government Code and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2017-01 and ensure that the total share on the additional one (1%) per cent tax on real property from the previous year be completely appropriated in succeeding year’s SEF Annual Budget to ensure funding of priority programs, projects and activities of public schools in the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender-responsive.  Likewise, use HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying programs and projects attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring its effectiveness.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD AR accurately and to submit copies thereof to the oversight bodies, including the complete accompanying reports such as: 1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) results of HGDG tests; and (3) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations;
Adhere to the approved GPB in the implementation of GAD programs and activities.  In case of changes, Management prepare a revised GPB for approval by the concerned officials and oversight agencies; and
Require concerned officers and employees to practice consistency and observe the guidelines in data gathering and reporting.  Make certain that the revised GPB is communicated to the concerned offices.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned personnel to submit to the Audit Team the required documents/reports as of December 31, 2021.

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the coordination  between  the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory  method  in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to  ensure  proper  accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and  materials; and
The Municipal Accountant and designated General Services Officer maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory, keep track the actual  Supplies  Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

We recommended that Management require:

The Municipal Accountant to ensure the submission of accounts to the Auditor within the timeline prescribed by Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise non-submission of the accounts shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and

The Municipal Accountant to request for relief from accountability for the loss of the said accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements particularly for Water System (Current Account No. 2782-1037-36), MIT (Current Account No. 2782-1048-05) and KC MT (Current Account No. 2782-1055-42) for CY 2020 for the immediate preparation of the correcting/adjusting entries for discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the financial statements and, henceforth, prepare and submit regularly the monthly BRS to the Auditor on time.

We recommended that Management implement immediately programs, projects and activities that may be funded by the financial assistance received by the LGU in order that the targeted beneficiaries may be able to avail of the services and benefits as intended by the donors.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Municipal Accountant to reconcile and adjust the amount in the ledger to reflect the correct balance as of the year-end. Also, to continue with the proper withholding of the correct premiums from employees’ salary; and

Municipal Treasurer to remit the full the remaining unremitted amount as of December 31, 2020.  Henceforth, ensure that the full amount that was withheld from employees’ salary, together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums be remitted to Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Appraisal and Disposal Committee to come up with reasonable valuation/appraisal of PPE items found at station and those acquired through donation which shall be recognized in the books of the Municipality in accordance with the guidelines set forth in IPSAS 17;

PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for each group of PPEs shall be maintained by the Accounting Office and General Services Office, respectively; and 

	Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to set up the correct depreciation reckoned from the date of acquisition/completion of the assets up to the present by debiting prior period adjustment and crediting accumulated depreciation and ensure that all depreciable PPEs are provided with depreciation.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive negotiate with the owners of the lots used as sites for the construction of SALINTUBIG Projects for the execution of the Deed of Donations in favor of the Municipality or concerned barangays;

The Designated Property Officer and Municipal Assessor prioritize the immediate registration and titling of unregistered/untitled lots acquired through purchase or donation in the name of the Municipality, thus, allocate funds for incidental expenses to be incurred in the registration and titling of lots; and

	The Municipal Planning and Development Council ensure that improvements and public infrastructures are constructed on the lot absolutely owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the LDRRM Council and Municipal Accountant to update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the programs/projects/ activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years; 

	Require the Human Resource and Management Office to fill up the statutory positions for Local DRRM Office prescribed under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to ensure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to investigate the discrepancy amounting to ₱68,212.58 and prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the Trust Liability-DRRM account.

We recommended the following:

Submit the following documentary requirements: 

Accomplishment Report showing computations of actual accomplishments as of September 30, 2020, of at least an average of 70 percent of all the targets under their programs/activities/projects approved in their LGU budget for FY 2020; 

Documents showing identifications/ listings of cost-cutting and systems improvement measures and justifications on how the available balances of MOOE allotments was realized and how computation thereof was arrived as a result of such measures; and

Observe strict compliance with the guidelines in the grant of Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) Incentive for succeeding years pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 dated November 4, 2020 and subsequent issuances pertaining thereto.

We recommended that Management to:

	Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion;

Ensure that the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and

Implement immediately all the projects under the continuing appropriations, otherwise, unutilized/ unimplemented PPAs which are no longer feasible should be considered for immediate realignment subject to budgetary rules or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20 percent Development Fund for reprogram/re-appropriation to other development projects.

We recommended that Management:

Include the above funds in the preparation of the CY 2020 LDRRMF Investment Plan and the status/utilization of the fund on the monthly Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and

Implement immediately the projects identified by the MDRRM Council to be funded from these donations so that the intended beneficiaries will immediately benefit from these projects, thus not defeating the purpose for which these funds were granted by the donors.

We recommended that: 

The Local Chief Executive, together with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Waste Management Board revisit the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan (CY 2014-2024) to fully implement the provisions of RA 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents; and

The MPDC, Municipal Engineer and MENRO Designate should see to it that all programs and activities programmed during the year are immediately implemented so that constituents may not be exposed to health, sanitation and environmental hazards.
We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit regularly Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles; and

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to work back and review entries to account for the discrepancies noted and make necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of affected accounts and remit any unremitted amount, if there is any; and

Make representations with the concerned officials of the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth as regards the settlement of the prior year’s unremitted contributions/ payments to avoid the imposition of interests, surcharges and penalties.

We recommended that Management:

	Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry (JEV) to correctly record the above funds as Trust Liability-DRRM (438) in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; 

Include the above funds in the preparation of the CY 2020 LDRRMF Investment Plan and the status/utilization of the fund on the monthly Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and

Implement immediately the projects identified by the MDRRM Council to be funded from these donations so that the intended beneficiaries will immediately benefit from these projects, thus not defeating the purpose for which these funds were granted by the donors.
observations.reason: Review of the approved CY 2020 Annual Budget disclosed that Donations/Financial assistance received in CYs 2013-2015  from various private individuals/entities for victims of Typhoon Yolanda totaling ₱0.926 million was not included in the CY 2020 LDRRMF Investment Plan and remained unutilized as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Sangguniang Bayan during the preparation of the Annual Budget for 20% Development Fund include only Programs/Projects/ Activities which are feasible and could be accomplished within the target date of completion, after taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership;

	Implement the development projects programmed during the year as embodied in the Annual Investment/ Development Plan and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund in compliance with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality

	Implement immediately all the projects under the continuing appropriations; and

At the end of each year, review all unimplemented projects and verify whether they can be implemented in the succeeding year. All savings from completed projects and all projects which are no longer feasible and cannot be implemented should be considered for immediate realignment, subject to budgetary rules or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20% Development Fund for re-appropriation to other development projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Appraisal and Disposal Committee to come up with reasonable valuation/appraisal of PPE items found at station and those acquired through donation which shall be recognized in the books of the Municipality in accordance with the guidelines set forth in IPSAS 17;
PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for each group of PPEs shall be maintained by the Accounting Office and General Services Office, respectively; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to set up the correct depreciation reckoned from the date of acquisition/ completion of the assets up to the present by debiting prior period adjustment and crediting accumulated depreciation and ensure that all depreciable PPEs are provided with depreciation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Local Chief Executive negotiate with the owners of the lots used as sites for the construction of SALINTUBIG Projects for the execution of the Deed of Donations in favor of the Municipality or concerned barangays;
The Designated Property Officer and Municipal Assessor prioritize the immediate registration and titling of unregistered/untitled lots acquired through purchase or donation in the name of the Municipality, thus, allocate funds for incidental expenses to be incurred in the registration and titling of lots; and
The Municipal Planning and Development Council ensure that improvements and public infrastructures are constructed on the lot absolutely owned by the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the LDRRM Council and Municipal Accountant to update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the programs/projects/activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years;
Require the Human Resource and Management Office to fill up the statutory positions for Local DRRM Office prescribed under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to ensure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to investigate the discrepancy amounting to ₱68,212.58 and prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the Trust Liability-DRRM account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all remedies to recover records/documents available to validate claims or else file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Submit the following documentary requirements:
Accomplishment Report showing computations of actual accomplishments as of September 30, 2020, of at least an average of 70 percent of all the targets under their programs/activities/projects approved in their LGU budget for FY 2020;
Documents showing identifications/listings of cost-cutting and systems improvement measures and justifications on how the available balances of MOOE allotments was realized and how computation thereof was arrived as a result of such measures; and
Observe strict compliance with the guidelines in the grant of Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) Incentive for succeeding years pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 dated November 4, 2020 and subsequent issuances pertaining thereto.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion;
Ensure that the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and
Implement immediately all the projects under the continuing appropriations, otherwise, unutilized/unimplemented PPAs which are no longer feasible should be considered for immediate realignment subject to budgetary rules or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20 percent Development Fund for reprogram/re-appropriation to other development projects.

We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Accountant to ensure the submission of accounts to the Auditor within the timeline prescribed by Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise non-submission of the accounts shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and
The Municipal Accountant to request for relief from accountability for the loss of the said accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the Cash-in Bank-Local Currency, Current Accounts, and record the necessary adjustments.  Further, require all accountable officers to update their cashbooks daily.
Henceforth, the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their books of accounts, at least quarterly, to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the bank accounts for CY 2020 for the immediate preparation of the correcting/adjusting entries for discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the financial statements and, henceforth, prepare and submit regularly the monthly BRS to the Auditor on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed analysis on the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and its related Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts and make the necessary adjustments to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Assessor submit the assessment roll to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer at year-end as basis of the latter in the preparation of the RPT Collectibles for the year;
The Acting Municipal Treasurer update the RPTAR /Taxpayer’s index card based on the Report of RPT Collectibles for year which was also based on the Assessment Roll of the preceeding year and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the correct certified list of taxes due and collectible as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income accounts; and
The Municipal Accountant: (i) review/reconcile the certified list before setting up the RPT and SET receivable accounts for the year; and (ii) avoid using estimated amount in setting-up the RPT/SET receivable for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the transactions and reconcile the difference of ₱304,695 in the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds as of December 31, 2020.

We recommended that Management request for the write-off of receivable accounts which have been dormant for ten years or more, after all the remedies have been exhausted, following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned personnel to submit to the Audit Team the aging of receivables for the Trust Fund as of December 31, 2020.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Inventory Team to conduct a thorough and complete inventory-taking of all the properties of the Municipality, prepare the RPCPPE  classified per account type, reconcile the same with the accounting records and submit a copy thereof  to the Audit Team for audit purposes;
Direct the OIC-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to continue updating its stock/property cards and ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation of property  and accounting records and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute for the depreciation of these assets and to record the same in the books of accounts to allow the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the year-end financial statements

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The General Service Officer to: (a) Render Road Map; and, (b) Keep a complete and updated Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs) for all roads and its components;
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and,
The Municipal Accountant to: (a) Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components; (b) Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLCs); (c) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; (d) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and (e) Include the necessary disclosure on the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned personnel to submit to the Audit Team the required documents/reports as of December 31, 2020.
We recommended that the Municipal Government fully comply with the reporting requirements prescribed in Item 4 of DBM LBC No. 125 for the BGCM funds received.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the mandatory posting requirements for emergency procurements undertaken by the Municipality pursuant to the Bayanihan Act to uphold transparency and accountability in the procurement transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO to coordinate with the MSWDO and prepare the following reports and submit to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009, to wit:
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations;
Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; and
Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of In-kind Donations

We recommended that Management:
Submit to the Audit Team the appropriate payrolls and other lacking supporting documents for the Social Amelioration Program-Emergency Subsidy Program (SAP-ESP);
Ensure that disbursements of funds are properly supported with the complete required supporting documents; and
In future subsidies, strictly comply with the Memorandum Circular to ensure that the subsidies are actually received by the targeted beneficiaries, thereby, achieving the objectives of the Emergency Subsidy Program of the government.

We recommended that Management implement immediately programs, projects and activities that may be funded by the financial assistance received by the LGU in order that the targeted beneficiaries may be able to avail of the services and benefits as intended by the donors.

We recommended that Management require the concerend personnel the immedicate submission of the lacking documents such as: (a) Distribution List; (b) BAC Resolution recommending the award of the Contract; (c) Proof of posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation in the PhilGEPS; (d) Schedule of goods offered by the supplier; (e) Bid Securing Declaration Form; and (f) Sworn affidavit of the bidder that it is not related to the HOPE by consaguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree.  Non-submission of the said documents shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the LDRRMF in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to provide the LSB the certified statement containing the following reports, for the latter to properly compute the amount available for appropriation, namely: (a) actual SEF Income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; (b) actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; (c) the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and (d) the annual estimates for the ensuing year;
The LSB the early submission of the complete School Improvement Plans by the school heads, indicating therein the priority needs of the schools based on DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017, together with budget matrix showing its sources, in order that the same may be considered in the preparation of the SEF Budget.
The LSB formulate/prepare the required three-year strategic program and SIPs which are necessary input in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and also furnish the Audit Team of the same for reference and review purposes; and
The LSB to strictly adhere to the prescribed formula in the computation of the available appropriation for SEF.

We commended the Municipality for its GAD compliance on the submission of plan and budget to the DILG Provincial Office. We reiterated, however, our prior year’s recommendation that the focal person prepare and submit the required reports to be attached in the GAD AR such as: (1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) results of HGDG tests; and (3) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.
Likewise, use the HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned personnel, particularly the GAD focal person, to completely prepare the GAD AR based on actual accoplishments indicating the number of actual beneficiaries/attendees of the implemented PPAs.
Likewise, revisit GAD PPAs and in case of crucial changes, prepare a revised GAD plan and budget in accordance with the identified gender-responsive PPAs  for approval of the concerned office/officials and oversight agency

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Continue with the proper withholding of taxes from compensation of employees and from payment to Suppliers/Contractor of Goods and Services; and
Remit in full the balance of unremitted taxes as of December 31, 2020. Henceforth, to remit within the prescribed period all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid the payment of penalties incident to its delayed remittance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Accountant: (i) continue with the proper withholding of the correct premiums from employees’ salary and (ii) analyze immediately the unremitted beginning balance of ₱135,390 in the Trust Fund books and remit immediately any amount determined to be due for remittance; and
Municipal Treasurer remit the full amount that was withheld from employees’ salary, together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Accountant to reconcile and adjust the amount in the ledger to reflect the correct balance as of the year-end. Also, to continue with the proper withholding of the correct premiums from employees’ salary; and
Municipal Treasurer to remit the full the remaining unremitted amount as of December 31, 2020.  Henceforth, ensure that the full amount that was withheld from employees’ salary, together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums be remitted to Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that after a review of the aging of Accounts Payable and other Payable, it can be ascertained that the Payables aging 10 years and above are already due for reversal to the unappropriated surplus of LGU funds. For accounts that are less than 10 years of age, the accounting Office, in accordance with other LGU departments, is trying to establish whether or not the Municipality is still liable for each payable. This is still a continuing undertaking.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and in accordance with the provisions cited in COA Circular No. 89-296;

Management create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee which will prepare the appraisal Report on the value of unserviceable properties and undertake the necessary action/s for the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and consequent depreciation in value; and

After the disposal, require the General Services Officer to submit the necessary documents, together with the Official Receipt to the Accounting Office as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The IIRUP as of Jun 30, 2020 was already prepared. This is an onset for the proper disposition of the unserviceable properties of the LGU. However, due to limitations caused by COVID-19, update of said report as of December 31, 2020 has not been facilitated.

The Municipality has already organized the Disposal and Appraisal Committee, as per Executive Order No. 27, series of 2020, to complement above said disposition of unserviceable properties.

We recommended the following:

Unified effort of the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Sangguniang Bayan be exerted to negotiate with the owner/owners of the subject lots for the execution of the Deed of Donation in favor of the Barangays. Subsequently, fast-track the processing of the transfer of title of the subject land in the name of concerned barangays so that the ownership to the properties would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes;

Implementation of any development plan on the property be deferred in order to safeguard the interest of the barangays. Further development should only commence once the TCT is already in the name of the Barangays; and

Henceforth, management should see to it that public buildings and structures are constructed on the lot absolutely owned by the municipality or the barangay.

The Municipal Accountant be required to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund and prepare the financial reports with necessary supporting schedules. Separate financial statements for the development projects shall likewise be prepared at year-end to form part of the schedule of the General Fund Trial Balance as mandated under Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 110 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and

observations.reason: Special accounts in the General Fund were still not prepared as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

The Local Chief Executive, together with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Waste Management Board revisit the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan (CY 2014-2024) to fully implement the provisions of RA 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents; and

The MPDC, Municipal Engineer and MENRO Designate should see to it that all programs and activities programmed during the year are immediately implemented so that constituents may not be exposed to health, sanitation and environmental hazards.
observations.reason: The LGU was not able to implement any construction, improvement and development of required facilities intended for the establishment of Municipal Eco-Park and Solid Waste Management Facility because the lot acquired has not been transferred in the name of the Municipality of Malinao. observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Assessor fast-track the processing of the transfer of title of the subject land in the name of the Municipality of Malinao, Aklan so that the ownership to the property would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes. Failure on the part of the management to immediately effect the actual transfer might result to possible questions or issues regarding ownership in the future; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the GSO Designate to verify the Monthly Official Travels prepared by the drivers of each motor vehicles and based thereon prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing the type of vehicle, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicles so that the use of the LGU’s motor vehicles can be properly monitored; 

	Require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit regularly Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles; and

	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all supporting documents for future fuel consumption are properly accomplished and submitted in order to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Verification disclosed that driver’s trip ticket and the monthly report of official travel and fuel consumption have been prepared, however, these reports were not submitted to the Office of the Auditor to determine the reasonableness of the fuel consumed for the period. Instead, these reports and documents were submitted together with the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Management comply with the limitations and regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in kind, currently set at ₱6,000.00 per annum pursuant to the guidelines set under DBM Budget Circular No. 2018-1 dated March 8, 2018 and Section 50 of the FY 2018 General Appropriations Act;

Stop the practice of granting of additional clothing expenses to permanent employees and those who are not employees of the municipality as this can be viewed as excessive and unnecessary expenditures as specified in COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012; and

Record the expenses incurred to the appropriate account title category as provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended that Management:

	Strictly utilize the LDRRM Fund for projects/activities enumerated in Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 to avoid issuance Notice of Suspension or Disallowance; and

	Replenish the Special Trust Fund – MDRRMF by the amount of ₱53,370.00.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and

	Ensure that adequate budget is provided for the establishment and maintenance of the GAD Database.

We recommended that BAC Chairperson direct the BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report of the Municipality and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and furnish the Audit Team a printed copy of the PMR to provide basis for monitoring the status of procurement activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator comply strictly with the requirements of Sections 2.1 and 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the submission of the required list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the posting  of signboards to facilitate monitoring and validation of the Municipality’s awarded and implemented projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned department heads to submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI so as to ascertain the agency’s compliance on the implementation of the recommended remedial measures contained in the CY 2018 Annual Audit Report (AAR), in conformity with Section 99 of the General Provisions of the R.A. No. 11260, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2019.

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is still on the process of investigation and reconciliation of balances pertaining to previous years. Appropriate adjustments shall be reflected as soon as the causes of unreconciled differences have been determined.

Henceforth, remit to the BIR within the prescribed due date, the total amount of taxes withheld, in compliance with the provisions of Sec. 251 of R.A. No. 8424 and  Section 4 of Revenue Regulation No. 4-2002 dated March 26, 2002, to avoid incurrence of penalties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the LSB and concerned officers and employees to ensure:
The strict compliance to the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2017 in the usage of the SEF; and

That priority programs and projects that are envisioned to contribute to the financial support of the goals of education be judiciously included in the plans and budgets for the succeeding years.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.  Likewise, use HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying programs and projects attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring its effectiveness.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant:

To properly maintain special account in the GF for the 20 per cent EDF, with complete SLs using the Sub-Codes for this Special Account; and

To prepare a post-closing trial balance and financial statements for this Special Account and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Government require the PPMP preparation by each end-user, its review and evaluation by the Budget Office for inclusion in the budget proposal, approval of the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE)/other oversight bodies of the budget proposal and the consolidation of PPMP by the BAC Secretariat into the proposed APP which shall then be approved by the HOPE for the efficient execution of government functions and in compliance with the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Designated Collectors of Malinao Waterworks System should intensify their collection from waterworks system to raise cash backing in order to pay their monthly loan amortization with the LBP as agreed upon and reduce their obligations as well as avoid possible penalties or surcharges for delayed payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that: 

The Local Chief Executive, together with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Waste Management Board revisit the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan (CY 2014-2024) to fully implement the provisions of RA 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents; and

The MPDC, Municipal Engineer and MENRO Designate should see to it that all programs and activities programmed during the year are immediately implemented so that constituents may not be exposed to health, sanitation and environmental hazards.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other information and to coordinate with the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and the Local DILG Field Officer on the generation and processing of the data in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01; and

Ensure the effective implementation of the Municipal GAD Database that contains systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to gender responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that management fully institutionalize the MDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that BAC Chairperson direct the BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report of the Municipality and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and furnish the Audit Team a printed copy of the PMR to provide basis for monitoring the status of procurement activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the Inventory Team to prepare the inventory report of PPEs classified per account type, have it reconciled with the accounting records and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the report;
Direct the OIC-General Services Officer and the Accountant to continue updating its stock/property cards and ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found. The updated stock/property cards could also be used in computing the depreciation of depreciable real properties; and
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover the cost of securing land titles.

Management Comment:
The MAO and MTO are exerting extra effort to reconcile the balances and have partly traced the differences.  They are, however, having difficulty with the prior year’s discrepancies. Management, however, guaranteed compliance with the audit recommendations on the continued coordination between the two offices, reconciliation of balance and recording of necessary adjustments.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the submission of the Year-end Trial Balance and FS. Henceforth, prepare and submit Monthly Trial Balances and Quarterly FS, together with its complete schedules and annexes, within the prescribed timeframe;
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the MAO of personnel from other departments, especially during report preparation and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of the mandatory financial report; and
Allow the Municipal Accountant to recommend qualified plantilla personnel to attend trainings or seminars relative to their work, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We recommended that the LSB strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, thus, as much as possible refrain from appropriating the SEF for the implementation of activities of school personnel, utility and operating expenses of the District Office giving preference to the operation and maintenance of public schools and construction and repair of school building, facilities and equipment.
Further, we recommended that the LSB judiciously identify priority PPAs for the coming year focusing more on the immediate needs of the schools and students.

We recommended the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the complete data regarding the utilization of SEF, particularly the status on the appropriation for the secondary schools.  Likewise, submit the quarterly utilization report within the prescribed periods as stipulated in Section 6.0 of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required three-year strategic program, taking into consideration the recommendations of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and other information from the DepEd RO. Also, for the LSB to furnish the Audit Team a copy thereof for reference and review purposes. Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in the planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines.
Further, we recommended that the following formula be followed by the LSB in the preparation of the SEF budget to determine how much of the schools’ fund deficiency shall be shouldered by the Fund:

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender-responsive.
Likewise, use HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying programs and projects attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring its effectiveness.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD AR accurately and to submit copies thereof to the oversight bodies, including the complete accompanying reports such as: 1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) results of HGDG tests; and (3) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations;
Adhere to the approved GPB in the implemention of GAD programs and activities.  In case of changes, Management prepare a revised GPB for approval by the concerned officials and oversight agencies; and
Require concerned officers and employees to practice consistency and observe the guidelines in data gathering data and reporting.  Make certain that the revised GPB is communicated to the concerned offices.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned personnel to submit to the Audit Team the required documents/reports pertaining to fund transfers as of December 31, 2019.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMFIP using the prescribed form and should include the projects and activities charged against prior years’ unexpended/unobligated balance as required in COA Circular 2012-002.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAOs for the LDRRMF for the proper monitoring and reporting of the Fund.

We recommended that Management, in the subsequent receipt of in-kind donations, direct the in-charged General Services Officer to prepare the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations and prepare/maintain an inventory list of all donated items.

We re-iterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management closely monitor and ensure the timely, effective and efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% LDF to help in the realization of social-economic and environmental benefits to the constituents.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to evaluate/review existing internal controls on the disbursements of funds for infrastructure projects to achieve reliability of financial reports, safeguard the assets and ensure compliance with laws and regulations.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and in accordance with the provisions cited in COA Circular No. 89-296;
Management create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee which will prepare the appraisal Report on the value of unserviceable properties and undertake the necessary action/s for the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and consequent depreciation in value; and
After the disposal, require the General Services Officer to submit the necessary documents, together with the Official Receipt to the Accounting Office as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry (JEV) to correctly record the above funds as Trust Liability-DRRM (438) in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;
Include the above funds in the preparation of the CY 2020 LDRRMF Investment Plan and the status/utilization of the fund on the monthly Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and
Implement immediately the projects identified by the MDRRM Council to be funded from these donations so that the intended beneficiaries will immediately benefit from these projects, thus not defeating the purpose for which these funds were granted by the donors.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, Municipal Treasurer to effect the immediate actual cash transfer of ₱2.090 million to the TF books and stop the practice of simulated transfer of cash to ensure that the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE balances are recorded using the appropriate accounts to preclude misstatements of the affected accounts; and
The Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancy in the setting up of the Unspent Current Year DRRM Fund amounting to ₱37,373.00 and effect the necessary correction on the submitted financial statements.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Government of Malinao strictly enforce the mandatory posting of warranty security by the contractors to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 of the IRR of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant maintain a list of completed projects as a reference for posting the required warranty security.

We recommended the following:
Unified effort of the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Sangguniang Bayan be exerted to negotiate with the owner/owners of the subject lots for the execution of the Deed of Donation in favor of the Barangays. Subsequently, fast-track the processing of the transfer of title of the subject land in the name of concerned barangays so that the ownership to the properties would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes;
Implementation of any development plan on the property be deferred in order to safeguard the interest of the barangays. Further development should only commence once the TCT is already in the name of the Barangays; and
Henceforth, management should see to it that public buildings and structures are constructed on the lot absolutely owned by the municipality or the barangay.

We recommended that Management direct the MDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years so that the Municipality becomes a disaster resilient community.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the amount of ₱5.112 million to the 20% Development Fund depository account;
The Municipal Accountant be required to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund and prepare the financial reports with necessary supporting schedules. Separate financial statements for the development projects shall likewise be prepared at year-end to form part of the schedule of the General Fund Trial Balance as mandated under Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 110 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and
Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer monthly the amount equivalent to 20 percent of the IRA to the 20% Development Fund depository account for easy monitoring of the fund and to safeguard it for possible misuse.

We recommended that:
The Local Chief Executive, together with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Waste Management Board revisit the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan (CY 2014-2024) to fully implement the provisions of RA 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents; and
The MPDC, Municipal Engineer and MENRO Designate should see to it that all programs and activities programmed during the year are immediately implemented so that constituents may not be exposed to health, sanitation and environmental hazards.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Assessor fast-track the processing of the transfer of title of the subject land in the name of the Municipality of Malinao, Aklan so that the ownership to the property would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes. Failure on the part of the management to immediately effect the actual transfer might result to possible questions or issues regarding ownership in the future; and
Implementation of any development plan on the property be deferred in order to safeguard the interest of the Municipality. Further development should only commence once the TCT is already in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GSO Designate to verify the Monthly Official Travels prepared by the drivers of each motor vehicles and based thereon prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing the type of vehicle, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicles so that the use of the LGU’s motor vehicles can be properly monitored;
Require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit regularly Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all supporting documents for future fuel consumption are properly accomplished and submitted in order to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended the following:
Management comply with the limitations and regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in kind, currently set at ₱6,000.00 per annum pursuant to the guidelines set under DBM Budget Circular No. 2018-1 dated March 8, 2018 and Section 50 of the FY 2018 General Appropriations Act;
Stop the practice of granting of additional clothing expenses to permanent employees and those who are not employees of the municipality as this can be viewed as excessive and unnecessary expenditures as specified in COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012; and
Record the expenses incurred to the appropriate account title category as provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended that Management:
Strictly utilize the LDRRM Fund for projects/activities enumerated in Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 to avoid issuance Notice of Suspension or Disallowance; and
Replenish the Special Trust Fund – MDRRMF by the amount of ₱53,370.00.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and
Ensure that adequate budget is provided for the establishment and maintenance of the GAD Database.

We recommended that Management fully institutionalize the MDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that BAC Chairperson direct the BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report of the Municipality and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and furnish the Audit Team a printed copy of the PMR to provide basis for monitoring the status of procurement activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator comply strictly with the requirements of Sections 2.1 and 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the submission of the required list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the posting  of signboards to facilitate monitoring and validation of the Municipality’s awarded and implemented projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned department heads to submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI so as to ascertain the agency’s compliance on the implementation of the recommended remedial measures contained in the CY 2018 Annual Audit Report (AAR), in conformity with Section 99 of the General Provisions of the R.A. No. 11260, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2019.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accounting Office to exert extra effort to account for the discrepancies and immediately remit the taxes withheld amounting to ₱0.673 million as of December 31, 2019 to the BIR. Penalties to be imposed thereof, if any, shall be the liability of the Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Accountant/Withholding Agent; and
Henceforth, remit to the BIR within the prescribed due date, the total amount of taxes withheld, in compliance with the provisions of Sec. 251 of R.A. No. 8424 and  Section 4 of Revenue Regulation No. 4-2002 dated March 26, 2002, to avoid incurrence of penalties.

We recommended and re-iterated audit recommendations of prior years that Management:

Require the Inventory Team to conduct physical count of the PPEs including the real properties. Thereafter, prepare a complete RPCPPE and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the report;

Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover the cost of securing the land titles of the 32 lots; and

Direct the GSO and the Accountant to update its Stock/Property Cards and Ledger Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found. The updated Stock/Property Cards can be of great use in computing the depreciation of depreciable assets of the LGU.

The Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the cash accounts for all funds and make the necessary adjustments thereon;

The Municipal Treasurer to regularly update the cashbooks per transaction and per bank account for all fund. In the absence of any transaction, the Treasurer’s Office should still update the cashbook, particularly at year end, and reconcile with the Accounting Unit. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall make the necessary correction/adjustment on the erroneous balance forwarded in July 2013 under the account TF - 2782-1000-79; and

The Municipal Accountant to record the bank reconciling items amounting to ₱5.66 million to correct the understatement of the account Cash in Bank-LCCA-TF.
