observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and every year thereafter, that shall be the basis of the latter to set-up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the accounting period to conform with Sections 19 and 20, Volume 1, of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs;

Provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of RPT delinquent accounts for recording in the books; and

Immediately post a notice of delinquency at the main hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay in consonance with Section 254 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and such notice may also be posted in the LGU’s website.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials/employees to prepare disbursement voucher and necessary supporting documents on all payments/expenditures, including remittance of shares to the barangays regardless of payment schemes applied.  Likewise, require the barangays to issue OR as evidence of payment received.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to follow the processing of ADA as specified in Section 4.1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2018-14.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the timeline for the release to the barangays of their share from the RPT collection that is within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the timelines on the recording of accounts and submission of accounts and reports to the Auditor, within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, as required by existing laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that Management look into the possibility of assigning/hiring additional manpower to the Accounting Office and to allow the Accounting personnel to render overtime service, subject to availability of funds, in order to enable the Accountant to update the recording of transactions in the books, prepare and submit the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management request the Sangunniang Bayan to pass an ordinance promulgating updated guidelines, rules and regulations on the operations of Malinao Water System, particularly on consumers without meter or with damaged meter.

Further, we recommended that Management formulate and develop effective and practical strategies that could enhance collections of all unpaid accounts and impose penalties/surcharges to defaulting lessees/consumers.

Furthermore, we reiterated prior year’s audit recommendation that Management earnestly consider electronic record keeping at the Malinao Water System for efficiency and easy generation of client’s financial records.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit to the LSB the complete and accurate data for the later to come up with the correct estimated appropriation for SEF in the ensuing years.

We recommended that Management require the timely submission to the Audit Team of all the required reports/documents pertaining to GAD for verification/evaluation. 

We also recommend that concerned officials/employees tasked to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report shall coordinate with the Accounting Unit to come up with harmonized reports.

We recommended that Management:

We also reiterated prior year’s rephrased audit recommendation for Management to reinforce the involvement and participation of barangays to the Municipality’s solid waste management programs especially on the proper disposal of waste and consider the establishment of more clustered barangay MRF as prescribed in R.A. No. 9003 for the remaining barangays deprived of said facility.

We recommended that Management closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the realization of social-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01 dated 22 February 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) strictly  monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts; (b) prepare necessary adjusting entries for transactions affecting the accounts; (c) prepare detailed aging of receivables once valid supporting documents are retrieved; (d) request for write-off of uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required by the Circular; and (e) issue demand letters or bill of accounts to those identified debtors.

We also recommended for Management that in addition to the manual recording of customer ledgers, to consider electronic record keeping at the Malinao Water System to efficiently keep records allowing easy generation of financial reports.

We respectfully recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal HRMO to update the Leave Cards (LC) on a timely basis to appropriately support the certified leave credits on the application for the monetization of leave credits  and stop processing request for monetization of leave credits unless the LC is updated;

Oblige the Municipal Accountant the meticulous review of supporting documents before preparation of vouchers to determine completeness of requirements in strict adherence to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

Compel the refund of excess monetized leave credits, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit disallowance.

Further,  we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) being the immediate and primary responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the Municipality to refrain from approving application for monetization unless such application are fully accomplished and claim properly and completely supported.

Furthermore, require the concerned personnel to cause the immediate submission of justification/explanation on the payment of monetization that are unsupported with updated leave cards and  without certified leave balances.  The non-submission of the required supporting documents to the claims shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended that Management require:

The contractors with existing contracts to submit clearance pertaining to payment of quarry taxes and fees for contracted infrastructure projects with the LGU, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Provincial Tax Ordinance No. 2001-05.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with Section 9.1 of Annex E of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 in issuance of work suspension to the contractor. Further, the scope of work of Municipal Engineer Office shall be limited only in the evaluation and recommendation to the Local Chief Executive.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the provisions in COA Circular 2009-001 by providing the Office of the Auditor copy of perfected/issued contract/purchase order and their supporting documents within five (5) working days upon perfection/issuance of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

Compel the concerned officials and employees to submit the justification on the deviations noted against the POW and to also submit lacking documents enumerated in the preceding observations; and

Require the Accountant to exercise her pre-audit function prescribed under Article IV, Section 474 that is to review supporting documents before preparation of vouchers to determine completeness of requirements.

We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of including the payments of honorarium of DCWs and OSCA and Gratuity Pay to CDW in the GAD Plan and Budget as well as in the Accomplishment Report and follow rules and regulations stipulated in the JMC 2016-01dated January 12, 2016.

Further, to require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel to cause the immediate submission of the aforecited lacking documents, otherwise, the non-submission of these documents shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance. Henceforth, to strictly comply with the provision of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 to protect the interest of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare, update and submit the monthly BRS, including the bank statements, paid checks and original debit/credit memos and other supporting documents, on all depository accounts of the LGU on a timely basis to ensure regular reconciliation of the balances of the accounts and prompt recording of valid reconciling items, in accordance with Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exercise due diligence in reconciling the bank and book balances and avoid inclusion of invalid/unverified reconciling items which casts doubts on the validity of the reconciliation process.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the cash transactions particularly the inter-fund errors using the appropriate accounts to avoid confusion in the recording of transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officials/employees strict adherence to the laws, rules and regulations on the implementation of infrastructure projects by administration stated in Appendix II of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, otherwise this may warrant the issuance of Notices of suspension and disallowance.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to stop granting cash advances without the necessary supporting documents and stop the practice of granting cash advance without specific purpose and allowing irregular settlement thereof

Comply with the requirements in the selection of person/personnel who shall be assigned to any special projects which requires superior knowledge or expertise in a specific field

Require all concerned officials/employees to submit lacking documents.

Demand an official receipt in the payment of any claims particularly those released to government offices.

Require the concerned officials and employees to utilize the Alinao Festival Fund raised out of donations/contributions received from other LGUs, civic organizations and private individuals in disbursing/paying for the expenses of fiesta/festival celebration/activities, before authorizing the use of other funds, if any.

Prepare a proper, complete and detailed budget proposal for the entire Alinao Festival with an overall Committee Chairperson who shall oversee the various committees.

Moreover, we recommend that resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguard against loss or wastage of funds.

We recommended that Management:

Require all the 35 payees who actually received the honoraria payments to immediately refund the amount they received, to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for want of valid and legal basis

Henceforth, the same honoraria payments should not be effected pursuant to COMELEC Resolution No. 10050.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the SL for the CY 2015 Cash in Bank accounts and furnish a copy to the Audit Team for verification.  Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete SL for the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account to support its controlling account balances appearing in the books, in accordance with Section 10 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the required documents pertaining to payments totaling to ₱2,257,475.00 to the Audit Team for verification, otherwise, the non-submission of these documents shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance. Henceforth, for the Municipal Accountant to process only those disbursement vouchers that are supported with complete documents in accordance with Section 4, Paragraph 6 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and concerned accountable officers to submit immediately to the Auditor all paid disbursement vouchers for CY 2015 amounting to ₱34,889,050.51 for verification and audit as required in Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Failure to submit the same, we recommended that Management shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission and Notices of suspension will be issued by the Audit Team for these unsubmitted 235 DVs totaling ₱34,889,050.51.

The Municipal Treasurer submit a Liquidation Report per cash advance granted indicating the details of the cash advance (e.g., nature of cash advance, period covered and amount), amount disbursed and balance, if any.  A copy of the paid payroll shall be attached to the Liquidation Report as supporting document.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Assessor prepare a schedule of fair market values of the real properties for enactment by ordinance by the Sangguniang Bayan pursuant to Section 212 of the Local Government Code.  Henceforth, the revised fair market values shall become the basis by the Municipal Assessor in the computation of the assessed values of properties.

Require all collectors to remit their collections daily to the liquidating officer; and

Deposit all collections turned over by the liquidating officer daily or as soon as collections reach ₱10,000.00 but not to exceed one week.

Information dissemination and confrontation with the water consumers/ households with the help of the barangay officials should be done in order to explain the give and take relationship between the municipal government and its inhabitants in the continuous usage of public utilities such as water system.

We recommended that the General Service Office Head/BAC Secretariat submit copy of all contracts to the Office of the Auditor with five working days from the date perfection and each of all the documents forming part thereof as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Monthly summary of leave applications of officials receiving RATA and the work schedules or performance standards/Internal Rules of Procedures of the Sangguniang Bayan for CY 2018;

Copy of the monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting Certificates of Appearance and Leave Applications, and a certification from the HRMO that the official or employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle.

Support the grant of RATA for the ensuing year with the following documents:

For regular officials observing an 8-hour work day, copy of the monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting Certificates of Appearance and Leave Applications, and a certification from the HRMO that the official or employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle; and

Specifically allocate HRD fund in its annual work and financial plan from where five percent of which should be tagged for awards and incentives in accordance with No. 7 of CSC MC No. 1, s. 2001.; and

Discontinue the grant of PRAISE benefits to all employees without adhering to principles or rules of procedure as this constitute irregular expenditure and the unauthorized use of savings from Personnel Services constitute illegal disbursement unless this is in conformity with Paragraph 2, sentence No. 2 of No. 6 of CSC MC No. 1, s. 2001.

Strictly and faithfully adhere to laws, rules and regulations on the grant of incentives to avoid issuance of suspension and disallowance as provided in Section 7.0 of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Budget Circular No. 2018-5 dated November 14, 2018; and

Valid and subsisting CNA; and

Evidence and documentary requirements that could establish and support the LGU’s compliance with the conditions stipulated in item 4.1.2 (d) of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Budget Circular No. 2018-5 dated November 14, 2018 which requires that the  accomplishment of the LGU as of September 30 of the current year of at least an average of 70% of all the targets under its programs/activities/projects approved in the LGU budget  for the year using the harmonized Results-Based Performance Monitoring System as mandated in Section 3 of AO No. 25 dated December 21, 2011.

Management ensure that at least one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) be allocated for the implementation of programs, projects and activities for the strengthening of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC); and 

The LCPC adopt a comprehensive plan on juvenile delinquency prevention and prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to the needs for integration in the Local Development Plan.

Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 27, 2007 and Section 2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in releasing government funds to NGOs/POs.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments to properly recognize fund transfers already made to NGOs/POs by debiting Due from NGOs/POs (1-03-06-330) and a credit to Membership Dues and Contributions to Organization (5-02-99-060);

Management require the implementing NGOs/POs to submit Fund Utilization Report on the Implementation of the funds received from the Municipal Government ; and

Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the proper recording and classification of the financial transactions in the financial statements of the local government unit as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

The Municipal Accountant to record the bank reconciling items amounting to ₱5.66 million to correct the understatement of the account Cash in Bank-LCCA-TF.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and every year thereafter, that shall be the basis of the latter to set-up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the accounting period to conform with Sections 19 and 20, Volume 1, of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs;
Provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of RPT delinquent accounts for recording in the books; and
Immediately post a notice of delinquency at the main hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay in consonance with Section 254 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and such notice may also be posted in the LGU’s website.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials/employees to prepare disbursement voucher and necessary supporting documents on all payments/expenditures, including remittance of shares to the barangays regardless of payment schemes applied. No. 7160.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the timelines on the recording of accounts and submission of accounts and reports to the Auditor, within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, as required by existing laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management look into the possibility of assigning/hiring additional manpower to the Accounting Office and to allow the Accounting personnel to render overtime service, subject to availability of funds, in order to enable the Accountant to update the recording of transactions in the books, prepare and submit the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management continue their efforts and activities that contribute to the attainment of the income targets while undertaking the necessary measures to increase the collections of income accounts that did not meet its target.

We recommended that Management request the Sangunniang Bayan to pass an ordinance promulgating updated guidelines, rules and regulations on the operations of Malinao Water System, particularly on consumers without meter or with damaged meter.

Furthermore, we reiterated prior year’s audit recommendation that Management earnestly consider electronic record keeping at the Malinao Water System for efficiency and easy generation of client’s financial records.

Henceforth, the LSB should strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned Joint Circular.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare and submit the required quarterly and annual reports to the oversight Department/Agency in the prescribed form and within the prescribed period and observe the posting of Utilization Report in the Agency/LGU website to facilitate effective monitoring of the allocation and utilization of the SEF thereby, promoting transparency and accountability.

Require the Municipal HRMO to update the Leave Cards (LC) on a timely basis to appropriately support the certified leave credits on the application for the monetization of leave credits  and stop processing request for monetization of leave credits unless the LC is updated;

Oblige the Municipal Accountant the meticulous review of supporting documents before preparation of vouchers to determine completeness of requirements in strict adherence to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

Compel the refund of excess monetized leave credits, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit disallowance.

Further,  we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) being the immediate and primary responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the Municipality to refrain from approving application for monetization unless such application are fully accomplished and claim properly and completely supported.

Furthermore, require the concerned personnel to cause the immediate submission of justification/explanation on the payment of monetization that are unsupported with updated leave cards and  without certified leave balances. We also recommended that Management prioritize the acquisition and installation of the waste disposal site and waste management facilities in accordance with Sections 41 and 42 of RA No. 9003.

Further, we recommended that Management reinforce the involvement and participation of barangays through massive information dissemination of the Municipality’s solid waste management programs especially on the proper disposal of waste and support the establishment of fully functional MRF for each barangay or cluster barangay.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipality hasten the complete rehabilitation of the closed dump site at Barangay Santa Elena and to fully implement all the solid waste management projects, programs and activities to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all depreciable properties periodically to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Creation of Inventory Committee.
Conduct of physical inventory of all PPE
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: 12)  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and concerned accountable officers to submit immediately to the Auditor all paid disbursement vouchers for CY 2015 amounting to P34,889,050.51 for verification and audit as required in Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Out of the CY 2015 235 unsubmitted DVs, 145 were to submit complete accounts to Auditor’s office in submission to COA

observations.recommendation: 14)  We recommended that Management require the contractors of infrastructure  projects implemented by the Municipality of Malinao to post a performance security covering the period from the time of the signing of the contract until the final acceptance of the project by the government as required in R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 012-2005.

15)  We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations on procurement. Post all procurement activities in the PhilGEPS completely and timely to promote transparency and competitiveness in public procurement for the benefits of the Municipality.

21)  Strict implementation of the prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003 on the solid waste segregation at source, establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities (MRF)in the barangays; and,
22)  Intensify the SWM information dissemination campaign and enforce all regulations to the extent of imposing penalties for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction:
Implementation  of the no segregation/no collection policy.
Expedite negotiation with tenants/land lords on the acquisition of land for SWM use.
observations.reason: Partially Acquisition of land implemented still under negotiation with tenants/land lords on the acquisition of land for SWM use.

observations.recommendation: 31) We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately the disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accountant for recording who shall, thereafter, transmit the same to the Auditor for review and post-audit.

32) We recommended that management ensure that the remaining projects that were not implemented in CY 2014 be completed in the succeeding year in order that the benefits thereof may be enjoyed by the targeted beneficiaries.

33) Henceforth, management ensure the efficient and economical implementation of projects funded by the 20% local development fund in order to achieve social, economic and environmental development of the municipality of Malinao.
observations.managementAction:
Partially implemented
The LCE  shall require the Municipal Treasurer to submit complete accounts to Accounting Unit for submission to COA
observations.reason: Out of the CY 2015 235 unsubmitted DVs, 145 were to submit complete accounts to Auditor’s office in submission to the Auditor’s office in submission to COA. Further, the lack of complete information in the DVs hindered establishing the accuracy of the reports.

observations.recommendation:
37)  The collectors shall remit their collections to the Municipal Treasurer daily.

38)  The Municipal Treasurer shall deposit intact the collections remitted to her by the collectors daily or as soon as the accumulated amount reaches $£500.00 considering that the Municipality’s depository bank is less than 15 kms. Away.

39)  The Municipal Treasurer shall accomplish and provide completely the details required in her RCD, including the name/s of collector/s and the respective RCD number/s of the collections she is depositing to facilitate verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer shall issue memorandum to all Collectors

observations.recommendation: 40) We recommended that local government shall comply with the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 in the procurement of ordinary and regular office supplies and equipment.
